content.problemStatement:
Given an integer **N** representing the length of the array, followed by **N elements** of the array,Find the longest subarray that does not contain zero.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the length of the input array.
Second line of input contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output on a single line - the length of longest non-zero subarray.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the length of the longest contiguous subarray that does not contain the value zero.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array contains three non‑zero elements each isolated by zeros, so the longest subarray without a zero has length 1.
After the zero at position 3 the subarray [3,4,5] is the longest contiguous segment without a zero, length 3.
All elements are zero, therefore there is no non‑zero subarray; the answer is 0.

solution.approach: We scan the array once while maintaining the length of the current segment of non‑zero elements (cur). Whenever we encounter a zero we reset cur to 0. After processing each element we update the maximum length seen so far (maxLen). This greedy linear scan yields the answer because any longest zero‑free subarray must be a maximal block of consecutive non‑zero values, which is exactly what we track.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int n; scanf("%d",&n); int maxLen=0,cur=0; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int x; scanf("%d",&x); if(x==0) cur=0; else cur++; if(cur>maxLen) maxLen=cur;} printf("%d\n",maxLen);} return 0; }
